title: oenothera polymorpha var. undulata
intro: Oenothera polymorpha var. undulata, also known as California evening primrose, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the Onagraceae family. It is a type of evening primrose that is native to the western regions of North America, characterized by its distinctive undulate leaves and showy, fragrant flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been shown to provide relief from wounds, skin irritations, and minor injuries. California evening primrose can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and salves, which are believed to offer numerous health benefits, including soothing skin conditions, reducing inflammation, and promoting wound healing.
